commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=962de1b78b3548f542de9ae1a6e24053862b3eda)
commands.review_summary:
The PR exposes the concrete requester session key plus a `sessions_send` return instruction in the initial A2A target prompt, keeps ping-pong and announce contexts placeholdered, and updates focused tests.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +57. Total +68 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main starts the A2A target run with `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"`, while the PR adds a prompt instruction that steers the target through reverse `sessions_send`. I did not run a live WhatsApp/A2A reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow repair can replace the reverse-send prompt instruction with the existing source-reply delivery contract and update the focused tests.

target.title: fix: expose sessions_send requester return path
target.body:
## Summary
- expose the concrete requester session key only in the initial `sessions_send` A2A target prompt as an explicit return-path instruction
- keep shared ping-pong reply and final announce session lines placeholdered to avoid high-cardinality requester-key exposure outside the first target turn
- keep target session keys placeholdered and add regression coverage for WhatsApp group return routing plus requester-side self-route prevention

## Why
A WhatsApp group-originated Koro 2 request to Alfred lost its original return path. Alfred received placeholder context (`<REQUESTER_SESSION>`) and sent the result to `agent:koro:main` / internal UI instead of the originating WhatsApp group session (`agent:koro:whatsapp:group:120363426513385961@g.us`).

## Verification
- RED: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions-send-helpers.test.ts` failed on the missing concrete requester session before the fix.
- GREEN: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions-send-helpers.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/agent-step.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` passed 5 files / 108 tests after the scoped follow-up commit.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Real Behavior Proof
Redacted live relay test on 2026-06-27:
- Source requester session: `agent:koro:whatsapp:group:120363426513385961@g.us`.
- Target session: `agent:alfred:main`.
- Alfred main resent the Sandweiss SAT packet back to the exact requester session using `sessions_send`.
- Koro 2 posted visibly to the originating WhatsApp group exactly once.
- Gateway evidence: WhatsApp group `120363426513385961@g.us`, message id `3EB02408D91490BA94EE86`.
- No self-route to `agent:koro:main` was observed in the relay closeout.

## Review
ClawSweeper flagged that the first version made requester session lines concrete through the shared helper, which also feeds ping-pong reply and announce contexts. Follow-up commit `962de1b78b` scopes the concrete requester session to `includeRequesterReturnInstruction` only, so the shared reply/announce contexts keep `<REQUESTER_SESSION>` while the initial target prompt still receives the concrete return-path instruction.

## Risk
The requester session key is visible to the first A2A target run only so the target can return to the originating visible session. Reply and announce contexts remain placeholdered to reduce prompt churn and avoid requester-side self-routing hints.
target.author: henryyuen168-ship-it
